groups.notes: Users report genuine stress relief and improved sleep. Premium trial converts well — discount framing resonates. Strong candidate for testimonial sourcing.

groups.notes: High-intent users blocked by cluttered navigation — even therapist-referred users abandon sessions. Key pain points: search, soundscape discovery, too many steps to reach content. Priority UX fix.

example_reviews: hid lacklustre content behind obligation create account plu stuff expensive hell hard pas thank
four star content two star usability
groups.notes: Users distinguish content quality from usability — content itself gets praise but is gated too aggressively. Account creation friction before value delivery is a recurring complaint. Consider a stronger free preview.

up until two day ago app work fine tablet now its try open then crash error message close app ive uninstall re install work heeelllppp
groups.notes: Crashes, unexpected ads shown to paid users, and tablet-specific instability are the dominant issues. Ads appearing for premium subscribers is a trust-breaker and likely driving downgrades. Needs urgent QA pass.

groups.theme: Subscription, billing, and free trial concerns
example_reviews: read one star review decide want try free version noth want them steal money

normally take money from customer give them someth other than headache stole money from wife wont reinstate account refund whole year subscription taken cant us customer support has been contact multiple time keep ask same information again again try get us stop communication can just keep money frustrat when their support team oversea too every message one two hour wast before get any reply
groups.notes: Most severe sentiment group — allegations of unauthorized charges and unresponsive support. Repeated information requests from support perceived as stonewalling. Reputational risk is high; refund and escalation policy review recommended.
